zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster於大約1440年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德波內希德時，他的父親Sir Robert Bellingham42歲，母親Elisabeth Tunstall44歲。他於大約1459年與Joan Wiltshire在英國素可萊明斯特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1490年3月25日在英國素可萊明斯特享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster于大约1440年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德波內希德时，他的父亲Sir Robert Bellingham42岁，母亲Elisabeth Tunstall44岁。他于大约1459年与Joan Wiltshire在英国素可萊明斯特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1490年3月25日在英国素可萊明斯特享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bellingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Bellingham, in Greater London (formerly in Kent) and Northumberland. The former is named with Old English Beringahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the followers of Be(o)ra’, a byname meaning ‘bear’; the latter seems to have been originally named as the ‘homestead of the dwellers at the bell’, from Old English belle used in a transferred sense of a bell-shaped hill.

History: Richard Bellingham (c. 1592–1672) came from Boston, Lincolnshire, England, to Boston, MA, in 1634. He was a controversial political figure in the new colony, an opponent of John Winthrop (see Winthrop ). He was elected governor of MA in 1641 and again in 1654 and 1665–72. — An Irish family of this name trace their descent from William de Bellingham, who was sheriff of Tynedale, Northumbria, in 1279. They were established in Ireland by Robert Bellingham, who settled in County Longford in 1611. They gave their name to Castlebellingham in County Louth, Ireland.

variants.name: Winthrop
meanings: English (Cumberland and Durham): habitational name from any of the places in Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire called Winthorpe. The former is named with the Old English personal name or byname Wine, meaning ‘friend’, + Old Norse thorp ‘settlement’. The Lincolnshire placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Wine + Old Norse thorp ‘secondary settlement, outlying farmstead’. The Nottinghamshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wīgmund or the Old Norse personal name Vígmundr + Old Norse thorp.

History: John Winthrop (1588–1649) was the first governor of the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He kept a detailed journal, an invaluable source for historians. He was born into a gentry family in Suffolk, England, whose fortunes were founded by his grandfather Adam Winthrop (died 1562) of Lavenham. In 1544 the latter acquired a 500-acre estate that had been part of the monastery of Bury St. Edmunds. John Winthrop was a Puritan who emigrated from Groton, Suffolk, to Salem, MA, in 1630 because of Charles I's anti-Puritan policies. By the time of his death he had had four wives and 16 children, the most notable of whom was his son John (1606–76), a scientist and governor of CT. His descendants were prominent in politics and science, including John Winthrop (1714–79), an astronomer, and Robert Winthrop (1809–94), a senator and speaker of the US House of Representatives.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster was born about 1440, in Burneside, Westmorland, England, his father, Sir Robert Bellingham, was 42 and his mother, Elisabeth Tunstall, was 44. He married Joan Wiltshire about 1459, in Lyminster, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 25 March 1490, in Lyminster, Sussex, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster est né environ 1440, à Burneside, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Bellingham, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Tunstall, avait 44 ans. Il a épousé Joan Wiltshire environ 1459, à Lyminster, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 25 mars 1490, à Lyminster, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster um 1440 in Burneside, Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert Bellingham 42 und seine Mutter Elisabeth Tunstall 44. Er hat um 1459 in Lyminster, Sussex, England Joan Wiltshire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 25. März 1490 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Lyminster, Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster è nato il circa 1440 nel Burneside, Westmorland, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert Bellingham, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth Tunstall, aveva 44 anni. Lui sposato Joan Wiltshire circa 1459, nel Lyminster, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 25 marzo 1490, nel Lyminster, Sussex, England, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Bellingham of Lyminsterが約1440年にイギリスウエストモーランドブルネシドで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Bellinghamは42歳，母Elisabeth Tunstallは44歳であった。約1459年にイギリススセックスリミンスターでJoan Wiltshireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1490年3月25日にイギリススセックスリミンスターで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster(이)가 대략 1440년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 버네시드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Bellingham(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth Tunstall(은)는 44세였다. 그는 대략 1459년 영국 수세스 림인스터에서 Joan Wiltshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 3월 25일 영국 수세스 림인스터에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster nasceu aproximadamente 1440, em Burneside, Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Bellingham, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth Tunstall, tinha 44 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Wiltshire aproximadamente 1459, em Lyminster, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 25 de março de 1490, em Lyminster, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster родился около 1440 г. в местности бурнесид, вестморланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Bellingham было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Tunstall, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Wiltshire около 1459 г. в местности лиминстир, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 25 марта 1490 г. в местности лиминстир, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster nació aproximadamente 1440, en Burneside, Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Bellingham, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Elisabeth Tunstall, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Joan Wiltshire aproximadamente 1459, en Lyminster, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 25 de marzo de 1490, en Lyminster, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años.
